name: Cova Tallada and Torre del Gerro Loop
name_original: La Cova Tallada — circular desde Les Rotes, Denia
region: Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni
description: Hike a moderate 2.6-mile loop to the unique Cova Tallada sea cave from Les Rotes, Denia, with stunning coastal views.
region_intro: Dog friendly hiking trails in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni are characterized by dramatic coastal cliffs, diverse marine landscapes, and views of the Valencian Coast. This headland, an extension of the Montgó mountain, features elevations up to 175 meters. The area integrates with Montgó Natural Park, offering a variety of flora and fauna alongside its protected marine reserve. Hikers can explore paths that provide expansive vistas of Javea Bay and the town of Denia. Best dog friendly hiking trails in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni The most popular dog friendly hiking route is Port of Dénia – Bous a la Mar Sculpture loop from Bosc de Diana, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views along the Dénia coastline. Another top favourite among local hikers is Carrer del Marqués de Campo – Dénia Castle loop from Dénia, an easy 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This route includes urban and coastal sections, passing by historical landmarks. Local hikers also love the View of Cumbre del Sol – Decorated Pine Tree loop from La Falsía, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through coastal viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Hiking in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni is defined by dramatic coastal cliffs, panoramic sea views, and paths within the Montgó Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 4000 reviews. More than 22000 hikers have used komoot to explore Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many dog-friendly hiking trails are available in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni?
A: There are 8 dog-friendly hiking trails in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni. Most of these, 7 out of 8, are rated as easy, making them suitable for a wide range of hikers and their canine companions.

Q: Are there easy dog-friendly routes for beginners or families with dogs?
A: Yes, the region offers a great selection of easy dog-friendly routes perfect for beginners or families. Out of the 8 available trails, 7 are classified as easy. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14676603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrer del Piloter – View of Cumbre del Sol loop from Cap de la Nau&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 3.5 km long with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on dog-friendly walks in Cap de Sant Antoni?
A: Many dog-friendly walks in Cap de Sant Antoni offer stunning panoramic views. The cape is known for its impressive cliffs, providing breathtaking vistas of Javea Bay, the town of Denia, and even Ibiza on clear days. You can find excellent viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5931859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Cape&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4239473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cala d'Aigua Dolç&lt;/a&gt; along various trails.

Q: Are there circular dog-friendly walks in the area?
A: Yes, several dog-friendly routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14659769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Magazinos, Dénia – Dénia Castle loop from Dénia&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route that covers about 3.2 km.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions along the dog-friendly trails?
A: While hiking with your dog, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. The area features historical structures like the Cap de Sant Antoni lighthouse, in operation since 1855, and ancient windmills known as 'Els Molins'. You might also pass by natural attractions such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/299845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal path to Cova Tallada and Torre del Gerro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go dog hiking in Cap de Sant Antoni?
A: The best time for dog hiking in Cap de Sant Antoni is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, which might be challenging for dogs, especially on longer routes or during midday. Always carry plenty of water for yourself and your dog, regardless of the season.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly trails near Denia?
A: Yes, there are several dog-friendly trails conveniently located near Denia. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33750135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Dénia – Bous a la Mar Sculpture loop from Bosc de Diana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10353834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrer del Marqués de Campo – Dénia Castle loop from Dénia&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent options starting directly from or very close to Denia.

Q: What do other hikers say about the dog-friendly trails in this region?
A: The dog-friendly trails in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal views, the well-maintained paths, and the overall natural beauty that makes for an enjoyable experience with their dogs.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly beaches or wild swimming spots nearby?
A: While the marine reserve itself has strict regulations to protect its biodiversity, there are nearby beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5931868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Rotas Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5934888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marineta Cassiana Beach&lt;/a&gt;. It's always advisable to check local regulations regarding dogs on beaches, especially during peak seasons, as rules can vary.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of dog-friendly hikes in the area?
A: The dog-friendly hikes in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni vary in length, with routes ranging from approximately 3.2 km to 7.3 km. The duration can range from about 50 minutes to just under 2 hours, depending on the specific trail and your pace with your dog. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31518779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cumbre del Sol – Decorated Pine Tree loop from La Falsía&lt;/a&gt; is about 6.4 km long.
tips: Defensive watchtower from the 16th century. 13.5 m high, it is part of the network of fortifications built along the coast in the 16th century to prevent the constant incursions of Barbary pirates.
Very demanding descent. Surefootedness required. But great views and then down the cave: Cova Tallada

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated hiking time for this loop is approximately 1 hour and 24 minutes. However, due to the challenging terrain and the desire to explore the cave, many hikers spend significantly more time, often 2-3 hours or more, especially if they stop for photos or to swim.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail to La Cova Tallada?
faqs.answer: The terrain is rugged and challenging. You'll encounter rocky, uneven paths, steep steps, and sections that require rock scrambling. There are even parts where chains and knotted ropes are installed to assist with descent. The path can be very narrow with a steep drop to the sea, and surfaces can be slippery, especially with humidity. It's often described as a 'goat path' due to its ruggedness.

faqs.question: Is the La Cova Tallada trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally rated as having medium-high to high difficulty. It requires good fitness, sure-footedness, and caution. It is not recommended for young children, individuals with reduced mobility, or pets unaccustomed to mountain trails. The final section into the cave is particularly technical and can involve wading through water depending on tides.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The loop starts from Les Rotes, Denia. Parking can be found in the Les Rotes area, but it can be limited, especially during peak season. Arriving early is advisable to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular coastal views of the Mediterranean Sea and the cliffs of Cap de Sant Antoni. Key points of interest include the historic Torre del Gerro, the Trail to Cova Tallada itself, and the stunning destination, Cova Tallada (Cut Cave), with its clear turquoise waters and unique rock formations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be hiked year-round, spring and autumn offer more pleasant temperatures. However, be aware that access to Cova Tallada is often regulated during peak seasons (typically from Holy Week, May long weekend, and from June 15 to October 15), requiring advance booking. During these times, the cave can also be very crowded.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or reservation to access Cova Tallada or the Montgó Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, during peak seasons (typically from Holy Week, May long weekend, and from June 15 to October 15), access to Cova Tallada is regulated. You will need to book in advance through the Natural Parks of the Valencian Community website. There are no general entrance fees for Montgó Natural Park itself, but specific access regulations apply to the cave due to its environmental fragility and popularity.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's high difficulty, rugged terrain, narrow paths, and sections requiring scrambling with ropes and chains, it is not recommended for dogs, especially those unaccustomed to challenging mountain trails. The steep drops and slippery surfaces pose a significant risk.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: Proper hiking shoes or boots with good grip are essential due to the rocky and often slippery terrain. Water shoes are highly recommended if you plan to explore inside the Cova Tallada, as it may involve wading through water. Bring plenty of water, sun protection, and a headlamp if you plan to explore deeper into the cave.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. Approximately 69% of the loop follows 'El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana', and 14% is part of the 'Access to Cova Tallada' path.

faqs.question: Can I reach Cova Tallada by other means?
faqs.answer: Yes, besides hiking, you can also reach Cova Tallada by kayak or small boat. This offers an alternative way to experience the cave and its clear waters, especially if the hiking trail's difficulty is a concern.
